name: Axion monodromy inflation
summary: Generates a super-Planckian inflaton field range from a sub-Planckian axion decay constant by exploiting monodromy: the axion's potential is not exactly periodic but spirals through many fundamental periods, so the effective field range is N_periods x f >> M_Pl. Realises large-field inflation in controlled string-theory settings, producing observably large tensor-to-scalar ratios. Foundational for large-r inflationary phenomenology with stringy UV completion.

addresses_puzzles.note: Predicts r ~ 0.04-0.07 for nil-manifold (phi^{2/3}) potential or r ~ 0.05 for axion-monodromy (phi) — currently constrained by BICEP/Keck (r < 0.036). Tension with weak-gravity conjecture: the trans-Planckian field excursion may put the construction in the swampland, currently debated.

touches_benchmarks.note: Predicted r ~ 0.04-0.07 sits right at or above the BICEP/Keck bound r < 0.036; LiteBIRD will close the parameter space.
Predicts oscillating modulations on top of the smooth power spectrum from residual axion-periodicity effects; searched for in Planck data without confirmed detection.

new_symmetries: approximate shift symmetry broken by monodromy
introduces.note: Decay constant, monodromy scale, potential shape (Nil-manifold gives phi^{2/3}; axion-monodromy gives ~linear).

history: Silverstein and Westphal (2008), 'Monodromy in the CMB: gravity waves and string inflation', Phys. Rev. D 78, 106003, arXiv:0803.3085. McAllister-Silverstein-Westphal (2010), 'Gravity waves and linear inflation from axion monodromy', arXiv:0808.0706, generalised. Tightly constrained by BICEP/Keck post-2018 and by weak-gravity-conjecture arguments; remains a target for next-generation CMB.

references.citation: Silverstein and Westphal, 'Monodromy in the CMB: gravity waves and string inflation', Phys. Rev. D 78, 106003 (2008)
McAllister, Silverstein, Westphal, 'Gravity waves and linear inflation from axion monodromy', Phys. Rev. D 82, 046003 (2010)
